notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Sparrow (it never builds Storm Breaker, Spectra) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for sustained DPS: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +23.8% on this kit’s main output at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: On-Hit DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 398 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +26% more sustained auto DPS: try Viper over Storm Breaker (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: On-Hit DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 368 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +36% more sustained auto DPS: try Viper over Storm Breaker (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Crit Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 743 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 1% of our best core on one-combo burst. The winrate is earned, not luck.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Murdock deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite, at the cost of 41% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Revenant ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite, at the cost of 41% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Drongo deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite, at the cost of 41% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Skylar ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite, at the cost of 41% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. TwinBlast ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite, at the cost of 41% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Eden ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and armor blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 37% of its bite, at the cost of 41% of this kit's damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Sun Shards” ⇒ on-hit; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field carry 54.0% over 2,428 games, magnitude not simulated.
